_article: XXXIX.2 — grudges continue to fester. XXXIV.3 — grievances persist and spread. XXXIX.6 — a festering grudge can be settled by mediation (redirectable).
_description: Momentum schema for a Grudge entity (a first-class node in the relationship graph, per Article XXXIV.3). A grudge is not a static relationship modifier. It has a severity, a festering rate (rising unless amends are made), a flashpoint proximity (how close to open conflict), and catalysts (events that accelerate it) and decays (amends, mediation, shared threat). When a grudge crosses its flashpoint threshold, a conflict trajectory ignites — a feud, a raid, a war.

_design_notes.why_this_matters: Er Gen canon runs on grudges. Wang Lin's entire early arc is propelled by the Teng family's grudge against him and his reciprocal grudge against them. A grudge is not 'relationship -= 20'. It is a live trajectory that festers, spreads to allies, ignites under catalysts, and either explodes into conflict or is settled (rarely) through mediation or shared threat. This schema makes that trajectory declarable.
_design_notes.coupling: Feeds: conflict_schema (when flashpoint crosses, a conflict trajectory begins), rumor_momentum (grudges seed rumors), sect_momentum / settlement_momentum (a faction grudge may drive sect decline or settlement threat). Fed by: any perceived wrong (the event that seeded the grudge is in memory_ledger).
perceivable_without_notification_example: Two families in the village do not speak — the innkeeper mentions it without prompting.
A sect elder refuses to attend a feast where a rival elder will be present — the slight is public.
A merchant from one faction is overcharged in another faction's market — the grudge is in its economic phase.
Guards from two factions eye each other at a border crossing — the flashpoint is approaching.

schema.grudge_id: string — matches a Grudge entity id

current_state.party_a: string — entity id (faction, family, sect, npc)
current_state.origin_event_id: string — the memory_ledger event that seeded this grudge
current_state.severity: float 0.0–1.0 — current intensity (0 = grudge exists but is dormant; 1 = active conflict)
current_state.festering_rate: float — severity rise per tick when unaddressed (some grudges fester fast — blood feuds; some slow — commercial rivalry)
current_state.flashpoint_proximity: float 0.0–1.0 — how close to open conflict (1 = conflict imminent)
current_state.catalysts: array — event types that would raise severity sharply (a perceived slight, a border incident, a third-party provocation, a succession crisis)
current_state.decay_conditions: array — what would lower severity (amends made, mediation, shared external threat, generational turnover, mutual benefit)
current_state.spread_to_allies: bool — has the grudge spread to the parties' allies? (a feud involves more than the original two parties)
current_state.current_phase: enum — cold | simmering | heated | flashpoint | active_conflict | settling | dormant

rate_of_change.flashpoint_proximity_per_tick: float — tracks severity but also rises sharply on catalyst events

components.festering: float — baseline rise; higher for blood feuds, lower for commercial disputes
components.amends: float — negative contribution when mediation or reparation is occurring
components.catalyst_pressure: float — spikes on catalyst events; otherwise 0
components.shared_threat_cooling: float — when both parties face a common enemy, severity may decline (the enemy-of-my-enemy effect)
components.generational_decay: float — very slow; a grudge may fade over generations if neither side re-catalyzes

drivers: string — e.g. 'grudge_wang_family_vs_teng_ally: party_a=wang_family_village, party_b=teng_ally_merchant (a Teng-affiliated trader who cheated the village), origin=cheating_event_tick_12000, severity 0.62, festering 0.0008/tick, flashpoint 0.55 (heated), catalysts=[new cheating incident, teng_family_pressure], decay=[reparation, mediator_intervention], spread_to_allies=true (the village's allied families share the grudge)'

thresholds.name: simmering_to_heated
thresholds.condition: severity > 0.5 AND a catalyst event occurs
thresholds.consequence: grudge becomes publicly heated; NPCs from the two parties refuse to trade, attend the same events, or speak; rumors spread; allied parties begin to take sides. This is the perceivable 'cold war' phase.

thresholds.name: flashpoint
thresholds.condition: flashpoint_proximity > 0.85 AND a catalyst event occurs
thresholds.consequence: open conflict ignites; a conflict_schema trajectory begins (a feud, a raid, a skirmish, a war, depending on the parties' nature). This is the point of no return for this generation of the grudge.
thresholds.irreversible: true for this cycle (the conflict must run its course before the grudge can settle)

thresholds.name: active_conflict
thresholds.consequence: the grudge has become a conflict; the conflict_schema drives events (raids, battles, assassinations, lawsuits, trade embargoes). The grudge momentum is paused while the conflict runs; its resolution determines the grudge's next phase.

thresholds.name: settlement
thresholds.condition: amends_rate > festering_rate for sustained period OR mediation succeeds OR shared threat unites parties
thresholds.consequence: grudge enters settling phase; severity declines; current_phase moves toward dormant. A settled grudge is NOT forgotten — it persists in memory_ledger and may re-ignite if a new catalyst occurs. 'Forgiven but not forgotten.'

thresholds.name: dormant
thresholds.condition: severity < 0.15 AND no catalyst for sustained period (generational)
thresholds.consequence: grudge is dormant; living NPCs may not even know its origin (only the memory_ledger and the oldest elders remember). A new catalyst can re-ignite it, often with distorted details (the rumor_momentum distortion applies to old grudges too).

interruptible_by: Mediation by a respected third party (NPC or player with high relationship.trust from both parties; Art XXXIV)
Reparation (the wrongdoer makes amends — material, apology, or service; lowers festering, raises amends_rate)
Shared external threat (the enemy-of-my-enemy effect; lowers severity)
Catalyst event (raises severity — a negative intervention; may ignite flashpoint)
Death of a principal (may lower severity if the grudge was personal; may raise it if the death is blamed on the other party)
Marriage or alliance between the parties (formal reconciliation; may lower severity or may raise it if seen as coercive)
schema.perceivable_without_notification: string — REQUIRED (Art XXXIX.5). e.g. 'The Wang and the Zhou families do not speak; the innkeeper mentions it offhand when a traveler asks after both. At the spring market, the Zhou merchant was overcharged three times by Wang-affiliated stalls — nobody called it a grudge, but everyone saw it. The village head has been quiet on the matter, which is itself a signal. An elder, asked, says only: it started before you were born.' If empty, FAILS the Momentum Test.
schema.compiled_into: relationship_graph (the grudge edge, with live severity); feeds conflict_schema, rumor_momentum, sect/settlement momentum (if a party is a sect/settlement)
schema.canon_ref: EXPLICIT — grudges, feuds, and their festering are central Er Gen canon (the Teng family feud with Wang Lin is the canonical example); specific Chapter 1 grudges INFERRED (Art XV) or AUTHORED for simulation coherence
